title: Side Hutch for the Classic Storage Collection
summary: A tall open hutch cabinet with adjustable shelves and a crown-moulding top, built from a single sheet of 3/4" plywood or MDF. Part of a modular storage system.
description:
This is a tall, narrow open hutch — a component of a larger modular storage wall — built from one sheet of 3/4" plywood or MDF ripped into 15-1/2" wide strips. It's a simple box with adjustable shelves on pins and a crown-moulding cap that dresses up the top and ties it to the rest of the collection.

The construction detail worth internalizing is that the side trim does structural work, not just decoration. After you build the basic box (bottom and top fastened between the two sides), you wrap 1x2 trim around the sides and fasten it into the top, bottom, and sides — the plan says outright that 'the trim reinforces the joints on the hutch.' On a tall box made of sheet goods, that added stiffness across the corners is what keeps it from racking.

The shelves are finished separately and dropped onto shelf pins, so they stay adjustable — set them to whatever you'll store after the cabinet's built and painted. The crown moulding across the front top is the signature of the collection; mitre its returns cleanly. If you built from plywood, edge-band the exposed front edges before finishing. Designed for paint.

tools.note: Ripping the sheet into consistent 15-1/2" strips.
Alternative to the table saw for breaking down the sheet.

materials.name: Plywood or MDF, 3/4 in, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 15-1/2" strips for sides, top, shelves.

materials.name: Crown moulding, 2-1/2 in
materials.note: At least 24" of run for the top.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Fasten the bottom to the two sides, then the top to the sides, with glue and 2" fasteners. Check the box for square — a tall sheet-goods box that racks now will fight every later step. Use the plan's measurements to set the bottom position.

steps.title: Trim the sides — this reinforces the joints
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 trim around the sides, outside edges flush, fastening into the top, bottom, and sides. This isn't just cosmetic: the side trim reinforces the corner joints and is what keeps a tall box from racking. Don't skip fastening it into all three.

steps.title: Add the front and back top trim
steps.body: Fasten the front top trim (1x3), also driving through the sides into its end edges — use the 2" fasteners when going through the sides. Then attach the back top trim (1x2) the same way. This frames the top and gives the crown moulding a surface to land on.

steps.title: Add crown moulding, shelves, and finish
steps.body:
Mitre and attach the crown moulding across the front top — clean returns are what make it look bought rather than tacked on. Edge-band any exposed plywood edges, then prime and paint.

Finish the shelves separately, then set them on shelf pins so they stay adjustable to whatever you store.

images.alt: Tall open hutch cabinet with adjustable shelves and crown moulding
